DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-23-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MATTHEW GOLDBERG, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4285 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS LILAY, INC D/B/A INSTANT TAX, AND KEBRON DANIEL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO STRIKE - The motion to strike is denied and the demurrer is overruled. The complaint must contain: a statement of facts constituting the cause of action, in ordinary and concise language. (Code Civ. Proc., 425.10, subd. (a).) "While it is true that pleading conclusions of law does not fulfill this requirement, it has long been recognized that '[t]he distinction between conclusions of law and ultimate facts is not at all clear and involves at most a matter of degree. [Citations.] For example, the courts have permitted allegations which obviously included conclusions of law and have termed them 'ultimate facts' or 'conclusions of fact.' (Burks v. Poppy Construction Co. (1962) 57 Cal.2d 463, 473 [20 Cal.Rptr. 609, 370 P.2d 313].) What is important is that the complaint as a whole contain sufficient facts to apprise the defendant of the basis upon which the plaintiff is seeking relief." (Perkins v. Superior Court, (1981) 117 Cal. App. 3d 1, 6.) Here, the paragraphs that are the subject of Defendant's objections are appropriate to apprise Defendant of the basis upon which Plaintiff is seeking relief. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
